content: Just as a 2025 self-supervised world model memory agent builds its own predictive world representations from unlabeled interaction instead of relying on human-annotated training data, the open-ended conceptual development of the general term 'agent' across AI research can be reframed as a self-supervised world-building process: the research community collectively encodes new interaction patterns and unlabeled use cases into emergent, predictive conceptual boundaries for 'agent' without a fixed, pre-annotated definition. This reframing suggests that curiosity-driven active learning principles that boost performance for technical world model agents can also be applied to guide the open-ended conceptual evolution of core AI terminology.
